mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of monetary tightening, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple quarters, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates indicate robust labor market tightening and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside stagnant real personal income signal weakening residential investment and consumer deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during credit-constrained periods.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not predict negative 60-day returns when RSAFS remains stable, or the signal fails to maintain predictive power during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
